_meta.what: Mutation-score ratchet baselines (keeper order 2026-07-13: lock the campaign gains as only-up floors). One entry per mutate target; the scheduled sdk-mutation-ratchet workflow re-measures each and REDS below floor. Raising a floor is the only sanctioned edit after a measured improvement; floors never go down without a keeper ruling.
_meta.score: Stryker mutation score = (killed + timeout) / (killed + timeout + survived + noCoverage) * 100; compile/runtime-error mutants excluded

history.2026-07-13: seeded from the overnight quality campaign: permissions round 2 (92.87), openai dedicated rounds 1-6 (85.55); transport-anthropic seeded at 77.42 (measurement round, pre-kill-batch); transport-anthropic raised 77.42 -> 82.97 (kill batch 2, 14 tests); sessions seeded at 68.16 (baseline round); sessions raised 68.16 -> 71.24 (kill batch 1, 19 tests: tool-claims + store-adapter read fallbacks)
history.2026-07-13 sessions re-baseline (keeper: sessions gains ignoreStatic:true and its floor moves 71.24 -> 67.66 (de-noised re-measure, 14m47s vs ~40min noisy). NOT a quality regression: the old score counted 444 store.ts static mutants (module-load code) that any test kills by merely importing the module -- free points that inflated the package score AND injected load-variance noise across rounds. 67.66 is the honest behavioural-mutant score (post kill-batch-2: checkpoints 72.89, file-store 72.55, tool-claims 73.33, store.ts 61.43 = next dig target). Floor decrease sanctioned by the keeper ruling; from here only-up applies on the de-noised basis.
history.2026-07-17 loop-support seeded: ledger.ts (SCS-REQ-REPOS-01 R4, pure dedup-ledger logic) seeded at 92.12 after three kill rounds (72.41 -> 89.16 -> 91.63 -> 92.12; 186 killed). The 16 survivors are a documented equivalence class: unique-Map-key `<` vs `<=` ties, stable-sort 1-vs-0 comparator tails, JSON.stringify dropping undefined config fields, NaN cutoff no-ops behind removed early-returns, and unreachable guards.
history.2026-07-17 loop-support +retention: retention.ts (R3 retained-region store) joins the target at 96.55 after one kill round (86.21 -> 96.55: combined-overflow fixture + per-segment error-message pins); package floor raised 92.12 -> 93.10. Its 2 survivors are the 'utf8' Buffer.byteLength encoding-arg literals (Node defaults to utf8 — equivalent).
history.2026-07-17 loop-support +loop-control: loop-control.ts (R5 LoopControl tool) joins at 96.55 after one kill round (65.52 -> 96.55: the description/schema/ack strings are byte-pinned as model-side surface goldens, error paths and callback containment asserted by name); package floor raised 93.10 -> 93.73. Its 2 survivors are equivalence-class literals.
history.2026-07-17 loop-support batch-A fixes: T49 batch-A fixes (L62 joiner-aware cap, L63 faithful deserialize, L75 finite-at guard) plus their regression tests re-measured the package at 94.35 (ledger 92.59 / retention 96.77 / loop-control 98.28; 316 killed + 1 timeout, 19 survivors = the documented equivalence classes); floor raised 93.73 -> 94.35.
only-up bump after the optional-investment kill batches (permissions-mutation-kills-r3/r4, 53 tests) drove permissions 85.96 -> 87.58 (measured). The dominant win is the command-unwrap SECURITY core in rules.ts (80.89 -> 83.67): every COMMAND_WRAPPERS entry (sudo/env/eval/timeout/nice/…) now has a deny-passthrough kill test, plus de-obfuscation (\rm/"rm"), group stripping ((rm)/{rm;}), env-prefix, and the allow/deny asymmetry (a wrapped command never rides an allow). Batch r4 (SSRF WebFetch-deny arg field, git:* over-grant word boundary, MCP known-server longest-prefix, path globs) added only +1 net kill — those behaviours were already largely pinned by the existing suite, confirming the real-killable inventory in rules.ts is near-exhausted after r3. gate.ts (26 survivors, needs gate-setup fixtures) left for a later only-up pass. New floor 87.0 sits 0.58 below measured so the 0.75pp tolerance absorbs the static-mutant variance this target carries.
history.2026-07-20 transport-openai + permission: First DOWNWARD floor move under the only-up rule — sanctioned by keeper ruling A 2026-07-20 (ratchet discipline: floors move down only by keeper ruling). The 2026-07-20 weekly ratchet reds BOTH targets: transport-openai 85.55 -> measured 77.14 and permissions 92.87 -> measured 85.96. Root cause is NOT test-quality decay but FILE GROWTH: both floors were seeded on smaller files (openai dedicated rounds 1-6 on 2026-07-13; permissions round 2), after which v0.53-0.75 grew openai.ts to ~1626 mutants (image input v0.56 / error-normalization v0.57 / WV2-4 temperature v0.72) and hardened permissions/rules.ts (0.70.1 deny-bypass + batch-E), each adding branches without matching kill tests. T63 batch 1-3 (81 tests, openai-mutation-kills-r6/r7/r8) drove transport-openai 77.14 -> 80.28 (+51 REAL kills: decoder state machine, in-stream + non-2xx error classification, the 0.72.1 capability-degradation/temperature branches, message-encoding helpers). That is the clean (non-over-fit) ceiling: the remaining ~89 kills to 85.55 are dominated by idle-watchdog/backoff/debug mutants (the documented 2026-07-13 over-fit class) and translator-boundary equivalents (contentSeen guards only observable at the transport-layer empty_message check), and the batch efficiency curve (+18 / +29 / +4) confirms the killable inventory is exhausted. permissions was NOT kill-batched (ruling A re-baselines it as-is); its 127 survivors are largely rules.ts command-name StringLiterals (a data-table grind, recoverable later under only-up if the keeper invests). New floors: transport-openai 80.0 (measured 80.28), permissions 85.5 (measured 85.96) — each a hair below measured so the 0.75pp tolerance absorbs run-to-run variance; only-up resumes from here on the honest post-growth basis.
